session.tool_result:
18→ 19→**adding a new PR-dependent step to trigger-async-learn** → Read [Learn Without PR Context](learn-without-pr-context.md) first. Any new PR-dependent step must handle the None case from \_get_pr_for_plan_direct. The entire PR comment block is gated on pr_result not being None. 20→ 21→**adding a new filtering step to preprocess_session.py** → Read [Session Preprocessing Architecture](session-preprocessing.md) first. There are TWO preprocessing implementations: the exec script (preprocess_session.py) and erk-shared (session_preprocessing.py). The exec script has the full filtering pipeline; erk-shared has only Stage 1 mechanical reduction. New filters go in the exec script. Read this doc first. 22→ 23→**adding a new pipeline stage to the async learn pipeline** → Read [Learn Pipeline Workflow](learn-pipeline-workflow.md) first. New stages must be direct Python function calls, not subprocess invocations. The orchestrator uses tight coupling for performance. See the Direct-Call Architecture section in async-learn-local-preprocessing.md. 24→ 25→**adding a new setup path to plan-implement without routing through Step 2d** → Read [Impl-Context Staging Directory](impl-context.md) first. Impl-context cleanup routing: all code paths that set up an implementation context must route through Step 2d in plan-implement.md, which is the single convergence point for .erk/impl-context/ cleanup. Adding a new setup path that bypasses Step 2d will silently skip cleanup, leaving .erk/impl-context/ files in the final PR diff. 26→ 27→**adding branch_name to plan-header at creation time** → Read [Branch Name Inference](branch-name-inference.md) first. branch_name is intentionally omitted at creation because the branch doesn't exist yet. The plan-save → branch-create → impl-signal lifecycle requires this gap. See the temporal gap section below. 28→ 29→**adding dry-run support to one-shot commands** → Read [One-Shot Workflow](one-shot-workflow.md) first. One-shot dry-run mode must NOT create skeleton issues 30→ 31→**adding erk-consolidated label to a single-issue replan** → Read [Consolidation Labels](consolidation-labels.md) first. Only multi-plan consolidation gets the erk-consolidated label. Single-issue replans are updates, not consolidations. 32→ 33→**adding footer before PR creation** → Read [Planned PR Lifecycle](planned-pr-lifecycle.md) first. PR footer needs the PR number, which isn't known until after create_pr returns. Add footer AFTER PR creation. 34→ 35→**adding new agents to learn workflow** → Read [Learn Workflow](learn-workflow.md) first. Document input/output format and test file passing. Learn workflow uses stateless agents with file-based composition. 36→ 37→**adding plnd/ special-casing to cleanup_impl_for_submit** → Read [Impl-Context Staging Directory](impl-context.md) first. plan-save never goes through the submit pipeline — it pushes directly via push_to_remote(). No special casing for plnd/ branches is needed in cleanup_impl_for_submit(). 38→ 39→**adding post-dispatch operations without matching dispatch_cmd.py pattern** → Read [One-Shot Workflow](one-shot-workflow.md) first. dispatch_one_shot() and \_dispatch_planned_pr_plan() in dispatch_cmd.py must stay synchronized. Both use write_dispatch_metadata() + create_submission_queued_block(). Changes to one must be mirrored in the other. 40→ 41→**adding redundant branch-location guards to learn status checks** → Read [Remote Branch Learn Support](remote-branch-learn.md) first. Learn status checking in land_pipeline.py:341 requires is_current_branch or worktree_path is not None. Remote branches (is_current_branch=False, worktree_path=None) are not prompted for learn — this is intentional since remote sessions are handled via planned-pr-context branches. 42→ 43→**adding subprocess calls to trigger-async-learn** → Read [Async Learn Local Preprocessing](async-learn-local-preprocessing.md) first. This command uses direct Python function calls, not subprocess invocations. This is intentional — see the direct-call architecture section below. 44→ 45→**after plan-implement execution completes** → Read [Plan Lifecycle](lifecycle.md) first. Always clean .erk/impl-context/ with `git rm -rf .erk/impl-context/` and commit. Transient artifacts cause CI formatter failures (Prettier). 46→ 47→**analyzing sessions larger than 100k characters** → Read [Scratch Storage](scratch-storage.md) first. Use `erk exec preprocess-session` first. Achieves ~99% token reduction (e.g., 6.2M -> 67k chars). Critical for fitting large sessions in agent context windows.
... [25 lines omitted] ...
67→**calling `create_impl_context()` without checking `impl_context_exists()` first** → Read [Impl-Context Staging Directory](impl-context.md) first. Both submit paths use LBYL: `if impl_context_exists(): remove_impl_context()` before creating. Stale .erk/impl-context/ from a prior failed submission causes errors.

session.tool_result: docs/learned/cli/output-styling.md:159:When orchestrating multi-step async operations (like `trigger-async-learn`), use a hierarchical output structure to show progress clearly. docs/learned/planning/pr-discovery.md:19:Finding the PR associated with a plan issue is a cross-cutting concern that spans multiple commands (`get-pr-for-plan`, `trigger-async-learn`, `erk pr co`, `erk pr close`). Two fundamentally different strategies exist, chosen based on what metadata is available. docs/learned/planning/pr-discovery.md:27:| `branch_name` | Branch → PR lookup | `get-pr-for-plan`, `trigger-async-learn`, land, dispatch | docs/learned/planning/pr-discovery.md:50:## The `trigger-async-learn` Composition docs/learned/planning/learn-without-pr-context.md:10: - action: "adding a new PR-dependent step to trigger-async-learn" docs/learned/planning/learn-without-pr-context.md:26:The `trigger-async-learn` orchestrator resolves the plan's PR through a chain of lookups: issue → metadata block → branch name → PR. Each step returns `None` on failure, and the entire PR comment-fetching block is gated on the result. docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-learn-pipeline.md:5: - "understanding how trigger-async-learn discovers plan IDs for planned-PR plans" docs/learned/planning/index.md:10:- **[planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md](planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md)** — modifying trigger-async-learn orchestration, debugging why learn materials are missing or malformed in CI, understanding the local-to-branch-to-learn data flow docs/learned/planning/index.md:54:- **[planned-pr-learn-pipeline.md](planned-pr-learn-pipeline.md)** — debugging why erk learn fails for planned-PR-backed plans, understanding how trigger-async-learn discovers plan IDs for planned-PR plans, working on the learn pipeline docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md:19:**adding a new PR-dependent step to trigger-async-learn** → Read [Learn Without PR Context](learn-without-pr-context.md) first. Any new PR-dependent step must handle the None case from \_get_pr_for_plan_direct. The entire PR comment block is gated on pr_result not being None. docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md:43:**adding subprocess calls to trigger-async-learn** → Read [Planned PR Context Local Preprocessing](planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md) first. This command uses direct Python function calls, not subprocess invocations. This is intentional — see the direct-call architecture section below. docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md:4: - modifying trigger-async-learn orchestration docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md:8: - action: "adding subprocess calls to trigger-async-learn" docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md:20:Before PR #6460, `trigger-async-learn` uploaded raw session logs and let the GitHub Actions codespace preprocess them. This added ~30s of startup time to every learn run because codespace environments are slow for I/O-heavy preprocessing (reading large JSONL files, deduplicating entries, generating XML). docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md:26:The `trigger-async-learn` command imports functions directly from sibling exec scripts rather than invoking them as subprocesses. This is a deliberate architectural choice: docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-context-local-preprocessing.md:33:The direct-call approach won because `trigger-async-learn` orchestrates 6 tightly-coupled steps in sequence where each step's output feeds the next. The overhead of 6 subprocess roundtrips with JSON serialization/deserialization was measurable. docs/learned/architecture/subprocess-wrappers.md:253:Some subprocess operations should fail gracefully while others should fail fast. The `_get_pr_for_plan_direct()` pattern from `trigger-async-learn` demonstrates the lenient approach. docs/learned/architecture/subprocess-wrappers.md:302:### Real-World Example: trigger-async-learn docs/learned/architecture/subprocess-wrappers.md:304:The `trigger-async-learn` command uses **lenient handler** for PR lookup: docs/learned/architecture/fail-open-patterns.md:137:**Example**: The `trigger-async-learn` command needs PR review comments. See `_get_pr_for_plan_direct()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/trigger_async_learn.py`.

session.assistant:
## Phase 2: Enrich Manifest with Provenance Metadata
The plan says to add new fields to the manifest entry in `push_session.py`. The approach is Option B: compute stats in push_session.py before preprocessing. Let me re-read the current `_build_manifest_entry` and understand the stats computation in `land_learn.py`:
session.tool_result:
42→def _preprocess_session( 43→ *, 44→ session_file: Path, 45→ stage: str, 46→ session_id: str, 47→ repo_root: Path, 48→) -> list[Path] | None: 49→ """Run preprocess-session to convert JSONL to compressed XML. 50→ 51→ Args: 52→ session_file: Path to the session JSONL file. 53→ stage: Lifecycle stage (planning, impl, address). 54→ session_id: Session ID for the prefix. 55→ repo_root: Repository root for running the command. 56→ 57→ Returns: 58→ List of XML file paths on success, None on failure. 59→ """ 60→ with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir: 61→ prefix = f"{stage}-{session_id}" 62→ result = subprocess.run( 63→ [ 64→ "erk", 65→ "exec", 66→ "preprocess-session", 67→ str(session_file), 68→ "--max-tokens", 69→ "20000", 70→ "--output-dir", 71→ tmpdir,
... [35 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Now I need to understand what stats to compute. The plan says to use the same approach as `_compute_session_stats` in land_learn.py — which counts user turns and computes duration from timestamps. But to keep it simple per the plan's Option B, I'll compute stats directly in push_session.py by reading the JSONL before preprocessing.
I need to import the session schema helpers that are already used in land_learn.py:
session.tool_result:
227→@dataclass(frozen=True) 228→class SessionStats: 229→ """Preprocessing stats for a single session.""" 230→ 231→ user_turns: int 232→ duration_minutes: int | None 233→ raw_size_kb: int 234→ xml_size_kb: int 235→ xml_chunks: tuple[str, ...] 236→ 237→ 238→def _has_user_text(message_content: str | list) -> bool: 239→ """Check if message content contains non-empty user text.""" 240→ if isinstance(message_content, list): 241→ return any( 242→ isinstance(block, dict) 243→ and block.get("type") == "text" 244→ and block.get("text", "").strip() 245→ for block in message_content 246→ ) 247→ if isinstance(message_content, str): 248→ return bool(message_content.strip()) 249→ return False 250→ 251→ 252→def _compute_session_stats(session_path: Path, *, session_id: str) -> SessionStats | None: 253→ """Compute preprocessing stats for a session. 254→ 255→ Reads the JSONL, counts user turns, computes duration from timestamps, 256→ runs the preprocessing compression pipeline, and returns size stats.
... [25 lines omitted] ...

203→) 204→@click.pass_context 205→def push_session( 206→ ctx: click.Context, 207→ session_file: Path, 208→ session_id: str, 209→ stage: Literal["planning", "impl", "address"], 210→ source: Literal["local", "remote"], 211→ plan_id: int, 212→) -> None: 213→ """Preprocess and push a session to the planned-pr-context branch with accumulation. 214→ 215→ Preprocesses the session JSONL to compressed XML, then pushes the XML files 216→ to the planned-pr-context/{plan_id} branch, accumulating across lifecycle stages. 217→ Updates the manifest and plan metadata. 218→ 219→ Always exits with code 0 (non-critical operation). 220→ """ 221→ repo_root = require_repo_root(ctx) 222→ git = require_git(ctx) 223→ time = require_time(ctx) 224→ 225→ # Step 1: Preprocess session to XML 226→ xml_files = _preprocess_session( 227→ session_file=session_file, 228→ stage=stage, 229→ session_id=session_id, 230→ repo_root=repo_root, 231→ ) 232→

## Phase 3: Simplify land_learn.py
The plan has three parts: 1. Add a new `_log_session_summary_from_manifest` function for manifest-based display 2. Deduplicate learn PR creation by extracting `_create_learn_pr_core` 3. Add `_collect_session_material` that tries context branch first, falls back to local
